cross_scene_arcs.title: Awakening to Identity: The Search for Self
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Bower awakens with no memory and begins a fragmented journey to reclaim his identity, triggered by physical artifacts and interactions that slowly reveal his past and role aboard the ship.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Bower is reanimated with no memory, surrounded by dead crew members and disoriented.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Bower confirms shared memory loss with Payton and begins to accept that his identity is tied to a larger mystery involving time and stasis.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The cause of Bower's memory loss and the true duration of his stasis remain unknown, leaving his identity incomplete.

cross_scene_arcs.title: From Isolation to Leadership: The Descent and Ascent of Agency
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Bower transitions from a disoriented survivor to an autonomous protagonist through the crucible of the ventilation shaft, overcoming psychological collapse to reclaim agency and determination.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Bower is passive, relying on Payton for guidance and direction.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Bower emerges from the shaft having asserted independence, endured trauma, and redefined his purpose through willpower.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: His physical and mental resilience remains untested beyond the shaft, and his leadership is not yet recognized by others.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Revelation of Evalon: Love as a Driving Force
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Bower's emotional core shifts from confusion to purpose as fragmented memories of Evalon resurface, transforming her from a forgotten image into the central motivation for his actions.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Bower does not recognize Evalon's photo and feels no connection to her.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Bower is fully committed to finding Evalon, driven by love and regret, even at the expense of group objectives.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Evalon's current status—alive, missing, or lost—is unknown, leaving Bower's emotional quest unresolved.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Confronting the Truth: The Collapse of Mission Illusions
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Bower's understanding of the mission evolves from survival to existential responsibility as he uncovers the ship's true purpose, the extinction of Earth, and the urgency of resetting the reactor.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Bower believes they are crew on a standard mission with a return objective.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Bower accepts that humanity's survival rests on their actions aboard a failing ship, shifting from personal to species-level stakes.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Whether the reactor can be reset and what future awaits the survivors remains uncertain, leaving the mission's success in doubt.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Survival Amidst Betrayal and Madness
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Bower faces escalating human threats—from Hunters to unstable survivors—forcing him to navigate not only environmental dangers but also moral ambiguity and shifting alliances.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Bower trusts that surviving crew members are allies in a shared mission.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Bower confronts Leland, recognizing that danger now comes from other humans, blurring the line between enemy and survivor.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Leland's true allegiance and the extent of human degradation aboard the ship remain unclear, introducing ongoing risk of betrayal.

cross_scene_arcs.title: From Feral Survivor to Mission Guardian
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Nadia evolves from a feral, isolated survivor driven by immediate self-preservation to a committed guardian of the ship's ecological mission, revealing her deep knowledge and responsibility for the bio-vault and its purpose.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Nadia operates on instinct, attacking Bower to assert dominance and secure resources in a high-threat environment.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: She reveals her official role and long-term stewardship of the bio-vault, reframing her identity around the mission to repopulate Tanis.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The full scope of her authority and whether the mission can still succeed after decades of oversleep remain unclear.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Leadership and Authority Under Fire
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Nadia transitions from a distrusted outsider to a decisive leader whose strategic judgment is repeatedly validated, even as her authority is challenged by Bower and tested by escalating crises.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Nadia's navigational expertise is questioned, and her caution is seen as hesitation by Bower.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: She commands action under pressure, directs the group through the reactor bay, and re-energizes the mission when others falter.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Her leadership remains contested, and no formal consensus establishes her as the group's leader.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Fracturing of Trust Among Survivors
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Nadia's trust in her fellow survivors erodes as she faces betrayal from Leland and abandonment by the group, forcing her into deeper reliance on Bower despite initial hostility.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Nadia is physically attacked by Leland, whose intentions remain ambiguous but perceived as threatening.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: She is sealed below by Leland's betrayal and later forced into physical and emotional dependence on Bower for survival.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Whether trust can be rebuilt among the survivors or if alliances will continue to fracture under pressure.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Confronting the Truth of Extinction
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Nadia grapples with the psychological and existential weight of learning that Earth is gone, the crew has collapsed, and the Hunters are evolving, forcing her to resist despair and maintain the will to survive.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: She learns humanity is extinct, leaving the crew as the last remnants.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: She actively combats defeatism, insists on pressing forward, and reasserts purpose despite overwhelming odds.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The emotional toll of this knowledge and whether hope can be sustained in the face of total loss remains unaddressed.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Descent into the Unknown
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Nadia endures a harrowing journey from chaotic flight through a warzone to submersion in an alien ocean, culminating in her emergence into an unknown world, marking a complete transformation from survivor to castaway in a new reality.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: She flees through a rioting sleep chamber, hunted and abandoned.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: She surfaces in an alien ocean, alive but disoriented, facing an entirely new existence.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The nature of the world they have landed in, their ability to survive it, and whether they are truly alone remain unknown.

cross_scene_arcs.title: From Authority to Vulnerability: The Erosion of Command
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Payton begins as a disoriented but authoritative figure reasserting control after hyper-sleep, progressively losing physical and psychological dominance as his claustrophobia, memory gaps, and ethical compromises undermine his leadership. His status shifts from commanding officer to emotionally compromised and isolated figure, culminating in active resistance to truth and growing distrust from Bower.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Asserting authority despite disorientation and memory loss
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Defending a fabricated narrative to maintain control, eroding trust
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Whether Payton can reclaim legitimate leadership or has become a liability due to self-deception and psychological fragility

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Fractured Mentor: Relationship with Bower
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Payton's relationship with Bower evolves from hierarchical and functional to emotionally resonant and interdependent, marked by moments of guidance, deception, and deep psychological reliance. As Bower becomes both protégé and lifeline, Payton oscillates between nurturing and manipulating him, revealing the fragility of their bond under existential stress.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Mentor-mentee dynamic based on rank and necessity
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Emotionally dependent but ethically compromised connection, severed by communication loss
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Whether Bower will forgive or reject Payton upon learning the truth behind his manipulations

cross_scene_arcs.title: Memory and Identity: The Unraveling Self
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Payton's primary goal shifts from regaining orientation to reconstructing his identity as fragmented memories resurface. Each revelation—about the mission's true nature, his role as a settler, and the presence of his wife—challenges his self-conception, transforming his survival objective into a quest for personal truth.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Confronting repressed personal stakes, including familial loss and existential purpose
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Whether Payton will fully recover his memories or remain psychologically fractured

cross_scene_arcs.title: Truth vs. Control: The Crisis of Leadership Ethics
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Payton faces an escalating internal and external conflict between maintaining order through deception and confronting uncomfortable truths. His decisions to lie to Bower, deny Pandorum's role, and resist Gallo's claims reveal a leader prioritizing stability over honesty, setting him on a collision course with reality and rival authority figures.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Using deception to stabilize Bower's mental state
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Ideologically opposed to Gallo, clinging to belief in the Child amid systemic collapse
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Whether Payton's version of truth can withstand exposure or will collapse under pressure

cross_scene_arcs.title: Existential Collapse: The Loss of Earth and Purpose
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Upon learning Earth is destroyed and absent from navigation charts, Payton undergoes an existential crisis that dismantles the mission's foundational purpose. His response—shifting from despair to desperate action—reveals a struggle to redefine meaning in a post-Earth reality, merging personal survival with symbolic faith in the Child.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Shattered by the news of Earth's destruction
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Recommitting to action despite cosmic despair, anchoring hope in the Child
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Whether survival has meaning without Earth, and if Payton's renewed purpose is genuine or delusional
